extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked with 19km to go on Col d'Èze, distanced GAUDU by 50+ seconds at summit, won stage by 30 seconds
tactical_patterns: GAUDU attempted bonus second theft at intermediate sprint but backfired, losing 2 seconds to POGAČAR instead of gaining
YATES attacked same exact climb location as previous year victory, revealing predictable tactical preference that opponents can anticipate
POGAČAR's team rode tempo via KONRAD to catch break pre-Èze rather than allowing stage hunters freedom, prioritizing stage wins

rider_signals.GAUDU David: negative: attempted intermediate sprint bonus tactic failed, lost time instead of gaining, gap increased 12s to 14s to POGAČAR
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: attacked identically positioned to 2022 stage win, suggests limited tactical variation but knows effective move location
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: 55km from finish described as seemingly too far back to challenge, passive GC defense on decisive climb
rider_signals.GREGAARD Jonas: positive: prioritized KOM competition from stage 1, went in early break to secure first three climbs and jersey
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: turned final stage into solo time trial with 19km attack, 50-second summit gap shows complete dominance
